400.q: What is the Pixar A113 Easter egg?
400.a: The CalArts classroom number where many Pixar animators studied — appears in almost every Pixar film

400.q: What is the significance of the opening of Up?
400.a: A wordless 4-minute montage of Carl and Ellie's life — considered one of the most emotional film sequences ever

400.q: What is the twist villain in Frozen?
400.a: Hans — appears to be the love interest but is revealed as the villain

400.q: What is the significance of Toy Story 3?
400.a: The toys nearly face destruction in an incinerator — one of Pixar's most emotional moments

400.q: Who voices the Genie in Aladdin?
400.a: Robin Williams — his improvisational performance was groundbreaking

400.q: What is Soul (2020) about?
400.a: A jazz musician who nearly dies and travels to the Great Before, exploring what makes life worth living

400.q: What is Encanto about?
400.a: A Colombian family with magical gifts — except Mirabel — whose magical house is losing power

400.q: Who is Tamatoa in Moana?
400.a: A giant narcissistic crab who collects shiny treasures — voiced by Jemaine Clement

400.q: What is the message of The Incredibles?
400.a: The danger of suppressing what makes you special — and mediocrity vs excellence

400.q: What is Turning Red about?
400.a: Meilin turns into a giant red panda when emotional — a metaphor for puberty and cultural expectations

What real psychological theory is the basis for Inside Out?
600.a: Paul Ekman's theory of basic universal emotions — developed with psychologist Dacher Keltner

600.q: Who originally designed Mickey Mouse?
600.a: Ub Iwerks alongside Walt Disney — originally called Mortimer Mouse

600.q: What is the squash and stretch animation principle?
600.a: Objects deform when moving to give a sense of weight and flexibility — one of Disney's 12 principles of animation

600.q: What is the Multiplane Camera?
600.a: Walt Disney's invention creating depth in 2D animation by placing artwork on multiple planes at different distances

600.q: What is the deeper message of Zootopia?
600.a: A metaphor for racism — fear of the other is manufactured and perpetuated for political control

600.q: What is the significance of Bambi's mother's death?
600.a: Unprecedented emotional darkness for a 1942 children's film — the off-screen death traumatised generations

600.q: What is the significance of the ending of Toy Story 4?
600.a: Woody chooses to stay with Bo Peep rather than return to Bonnie — a bittersweet goodbye to the franchise

600.q: What is the Pixar Theory?
600.a: A fan theory that all Pixar films exist in the same universe in chronological order
